task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #23: [Feature] Agent Pipeline Status on /agents page (starting with Neo) ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/clawd/slideheroes-internal-tools` 2. `git fetch origin && git checkout origin/main && git checkout -b sophie/issue-23` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 23 --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run the project's lint/typecheck commands before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: [Feature] Agent Pipeline Status on /agents page (starting with Neo) (#23)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-23` 8. Open PR: ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools \ --base main \ --head sophie/issue-23 \ --title "feat: [Feature] Agent Pipeline Status on /agents page (starting with Neo)" \ --body "Closes #23 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." ``` **IMPORTANT:** After merging, always `git push origin main` — this repo deploys on push. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Create a new parameterized API endpoint that reads Neo's state files directly from the filesystem using established `node:fs/promises` patterns with graceful error handling for missing or malformed files - Extend the existing inline AgentCard component with a collapsible Pipeline Status section following the ChevronRight rotate-90 pattern used elsewhere in the codebase - Implement lazy loading of pipeline data when the section is expanded, with re-fetch triggered by the page's existing refresh mechanism - Use the established badge color scheme (emerald/rose/amber/sky) for status indicators to maintain visual consistency ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Backend — Pipeline API Endpoint Create a new dynamic API route that reads Neo's state files and returns structured pipeline data. This endpoint will be parameterized by agent ID to support future extensibility, but initially only Neo will return actual pipeline data.
Task 1: Define TypeScript Types for Pipeline Data Define the data structures needed to represent pipeline state and API responses. - Create type definitions for state file contents: `ActiveAcp` (currently running task with PID, label, issue, startedAt), `SpawnQueueEntry` (queued task with label, issue, queuedAt), `CompletionEntry` (completed task with result, duration, completedAt), and `CooldownState` (map of issue IDs to attempt counts) - Define the API response type `PipelineResponse` matching the shape specified in the ticket - Place types at the top of the new route file or in a shared types location if appropriate
Task 2: Create Pipeline API Route Implement the dynamic API endpoint at `app/src/app/api/agents/[id]/pipeline/route.ts`. - Create the route handler that extracts agent ID from path params - For agent ID "neo", proceed to read state files; for other agents, return a response indicating no pipeline is configured - Follow existing API patterns: `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"`, use `NextResponse.json()` for responses - Return structured response with `active`, `queue`, `completions`, and `cooldowns` fields
Task 3: Implement State File Reading Add file reading logic for each of Neo's state files with graceful error handling. - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/active-acp.json` to get currently running task; return `null` if file missing or empty - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/spawn-queue.jsonl` parsing each line as JSON to build queue array; return empty array if missing - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/cooldown.json` to get cooldown state; return empty object if missing - Read files from `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/archive/` directory, sort by modification time descending, take last 10 completion files and parse them - Use `node:fs/promises` with try-catch blocks around each file operation for graceful degradation - Follow existing JSONL parsing pattern: split by newlines, filter empty lines, JSON.parse each line with inner try-catch
Task 4: Calculate Derived Fields Add logic to compute duration and format fields for the API response. - For active task, include `startedAt` timestamp so frontend can calculate live duration - For completions, extract duration from file contents or calculate from timestamps if available - Ensure all timestamps are in ISO format for frontend parsing consistency
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase creates a new dynamic API endpoint that reads Neo's state files from the filesystem and returns structured pipeline data. Implement the API route at `app/src/app/api/agents/[id]/pipeline/route.ts`: - Define TypeScript types for state file contents: `ActiveAcp` (currently running task with PID, label, issue, startedAt), `SpawnQueueEntry` (queued task with label, issue, queuedAt), `CompletionEntry` (completed task with result, duration, completedAt), and `CooldownState` (map of issue IDs to attempt counts) - Define the `PipelineResponse` API response type matching the shape specified in the ticket with `active`, `queue`, `completions`, and `cooldowns` fields - Create the route handler that extracts agent ID from path params - For agent ID "neo", proceed to read state files; for other agents, return a response indicating no pipeline is configured - Follow existing API patterns: use `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` and `NextResponse.json()` for responses Implement state file reading with graceful error handling: - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/active-acp.json` to get currently running task; return `null` if file missing or empty - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/spawn-queue.jsonl` parsing each line as JSON to build queue array; return empty array if missing - Read `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/cooldown.json` to get cooldown state; return empty object if missing - Read files from `~/clawd/state/neo-loop/archive/` directory, sort by modification time descending, take last 10 completion files and parse them - Use `node:fs/promises` with try-catch blocks around each file operation for graceful degradation - Follow existing JSONL parsing pattern: split by newlines, filter empty lines, JSON.parse each line with inner try-catch Calculate and format derived fields: - For active task, include `startedAt` timestamp so frontend can calculate live duration - For completions, extract duration from file contents or calculate from timestamps if available - Ensure all timestamps are in ISO format for frontend parsing consistency ```
### Phase 2: Frontend — AgentCard Pipeline Section Add an expandable "Pipeline Status" section to the existing AgentCard component that fetches and displays pipeline data when expanded.
Task 1: Add Pipeline State and Fetch Logic Extend the Agen
